citation.title: Dynamic distribution of chemoattractant receptors in living cells during chemotaxis and persistent stimulation.

citation.title: Agonist-induced loss of ligand binding is correlated with phosphorylation of cAR1, a G protein-coupled chemoattractant receptor from Dictyostelium.

citation.title: Phosphorylation of chemoattractant receptors is not essential for chemotaxis or termination of G-protein-mediated responses.

citation.title: Switching of chemoattractant receptors programs development and morphogenesis in Dictyostelium: receptor subtypes activate common responses at different agonist concentrations.

citation.title: Control of cell type proportioning in Dictyostelium discoideum by differentiation-inducing factor as determined by in situ hybridization.

citation.name: Mol. Biol. Cell
citation.title: Constitutively active G protein-coupled receptor mutants block dictyostelium development.

reference.scope: MUTAGENESIS OF LEU-100; ILE-102; ILE-104; VAL-136 AND ASN-225

citation.name: Mol. Microbiol.
citation.title: Regulation of G protein-coupled cAMP receptor activation by a hydrophobic residue in transmembrane helix 3.

comment.text: Receptor for cAMP. Coordinates the aggregation of individual cells into a multicellular organism and regulates the expression of a large number of developmentally regulated genes. The activity of this receptor is mediated by G proteins.
Expressed predominantly during early aggregation and at low levels during later stage.
C-terminal Ser or Thr residues may be phosphorylated.
Belongs to the G-protein coupled receptor 5 family.
The gene for this protein is duplicated in strains AX3 and AX4. These strains contain a duplication of a segment of 750 kb of chromosome 2 compared to the corresponding sequence in strain AX2.

property.value: P:adenylate cyclase-activating G protein-coupled cAMP receptor signaling pathway
P:adenylate cyclase-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
P:cell surface receptor signaling pathway via STAT
P:desensitization of G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
P:regulation of G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway

feature.description: Cyclic AMP receptor 1

Helical; Name=7
Polar residues
Low complexity
Polar residues
N-linked (GlcNAc...) asparagine
In DN3; constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP.
In DN2; constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP; when associated with D-225.
In DN4; constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP. In DN1; Constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP; when associated with A-136.
Most substitutions increase affinity for cAMP and the most polar substitutions block development.
In DN1; constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP; when associated with N-104.
Reduces the affinity for cAMP by 100 to 1000-fold when associated with D-154; N-155; Y-156 and D-157.
Reduces the affinity for cAMP by 100 to 1000-fold when associated with G-148; N-155; Y-156 and D-157.
Reduces the affinity for cAMP by 100 to 1000-fold when associated with G-148; D-154; Y-156 and D-157.
Reduces the affinity for cAMP by 100 to 1000-fold when associated with G-148; D-154; N-155 and D-157.
Reduces the affinity for cAMP by 100 to 1000-fold when associated with G-148; D-154; N-155 and Y-156.
In DN2; constitutive phosphorylation and greatly increased affinity for cAMP; when associated with T-102.
No effect on cAMP-dependent gene expression, chemotaxis, aggregation and differentiation.
Greatly reduces ligand induced phosphorylation; when associated with G-302; A-303; G-304 and A-308.
Greatly reduces ligand induced phosphorylation; when associated with A-299; A-303; G-304 and A-308.
Greatly reduces ligand induced phosphorylation; when associated with A-299; G-302; G-304 and A-308.
Greatly reduces ligand induced phosphorylation; when associated with A-299; G-302; A-303 and A-308.
Greatly reduces ligand induced phosphorylation; when associated with A-299; G-302; A-303 and G-304.
Slightly reduces ligand induced and basal phosphorylation; when associated with A-325 and A-331.
Slightly reduces ligand induced and basal phosphorylation; when associated with G-324 and A-331.
Slightly reduces ligand induced and basal phosphorylation; when associated with G-324 and A-325.
Greatly reduces basal phosphorylation.
No effect on phosphorylation.
No effect on phosphorylation.
